title: Small Flower Pawpaw Identification Guide (Version 1, August 2022)
description: A guide I have created on how to identify pawpaws in general, and specifically how to tell common (asimina triloba) and small-flower pawpaws (asimina parviflora) apart from one another. This guide was created using public domain photographs posted by users to iNaturalist.org, which is also where I learned to identify these plants, by going through the observations and adding annotations, and learning what makes small-flower pawpaws different from others along the way :) I created this guide as a hopefully easy way for other people to gain that knowledge, and make it a bit easier for them to tell small-flower pawpaws apart from other pawpaws, and pawpaws apart from other species in general! This guide is public domain because I wrote it and I said so, meaning anyone can use it for anything they want, no credit or permission required, though I would ask that you let others know it's public domain, and they can use it too! I've uploaded every type of file I could think of, to hopefully make it as accessible as possible. Please feel free to download this, share it, print it out, anything you like with it! I want this information to be free for everyone to access! You can buy a print-on-demand version of this book here: https://www.lulu.com/shop/tinyel-/small-flower-pawpaw-identification-and-differentiation-guide/hardcover/product-kr2jz9.html?q=small-flower+pawpaw&page=1&pageSize=4 I set the price to be as cheap as they would let me, so it costs $21 to buy, and I get $8.50 profit. Please download the free versions first and only spend money on it if you already know the guide is useful to you! It's only 28 pages, so I don't think it's worth spending $20 on. All photos used in this guide have image descriptions, and links and the URL to their source. Some of them are little diagrams I created specifically for this guide, and they are also public domain :) If anyone is able to translate this into other languages, please feel free to do so! I hope this guide helps someone, and if it helped you and you have the ability, please consider donating to Archive.org so they can keep up the good work :)

title: Pixelated Audio 192
description: Sue Kasper, it's a name you might know by memory but if you grew up PC gaming in the 90s you're definitely familiar with her work. Sue was the lead composer on SimFarm and the international sensation SimCity 2000! Both games were developed by Maxis, a studio known for producing simulation games since the 80s including SimCity, The Sims, Spore and many others. Sue's eclectic tunes have been ingrained in the hearts and minds of simulation game players the world over, and there's no doubt that her work on SimCity 2000 inspired a great many other composers trying to capture that iconic simulation game sound. She's a lifelong musician and guitarist that's been playing gigs in a number of bands around California since the 70s. While we weren't able to speak to Sue directly on mic, we had an excellent conversation with Michelle sitting in as proxy for Sue to answer our collective questions about her work on SimCity 2K and SimFarm. It was a ton of fun hearing her experiences rocking out, and her work with companies like Maxis, Guitar Player magazine, and Avid/Digidesign. Music composed by Sue Kasper unless otherwise stated 0:00:00  Dawn of City (Sim City 2000) 0:06:44  Dancing Megalopolis 0:11:06  The SIM Magic: Experience Mode 0:15:04  Skycrapers 0:22:00  Tax & Money 0:28:35  (Excerpts) Tax & Money, Work Work Work! [with MIDI transcription issues] 0:31:18  Title Theme (Sim Farm) 0:34:13  Dead Farm / Debt Farm 0:39:15  Home Improvement (aka COWMAN) 0:41:53  Work Work Work (Sim City 2000) 0:48:25  Ultimate City 0:52:50  Key of Success 1:01:35  (Bedding) General Farm 1:04:12  Work Work Work! Composed by Sue Kasper Arranged by Ryuichi Katsumata for  Sim-Melody from SimCity 2000 Other things mentioned: MAGWest 2024  (Sep 27-29) MAGWest 2023 -  George Sanger Pool Party set ROCKRGRL  Magazine wiki All songs using Roland SC-55, thank you to Valley Bell for providing the rip!

title: TWIAR 1229 NAT
description: PODCAST: This Week in Amateur Radio Edition #1229 Release Date: September 17, 2022 Here is a summary of the news trending This Week in Amateur Radio. This week's edition is anchored by Chris Perrine, KB2FAF, Terry Saunders, N1KIN, Don Hulick, K2ATJ, Eric Zittel, KD2RJX, Will Rogers, K5WLR, George Bowen, W2XBS, and Jessica Bowen, KC2VWX.  Produced and edited by George Bowen, W2XBS. Approximate Running Time: 1:23:40 Podcast Download: https://bit.ly/TWIAR1229 Trending headlines in this week's bulletin service: 1. 23 Centimeter Amateur Band and The Radio Navigation Satellite Service Coexistence September Update 2. WRTH - The World Radio TV Handbook Continues! 3. 104 Year Old Ham is On the Air 4. 100th Anniversary of the Reading Pennsylvania Radio Club 5. Hawaii Big Wind Drill Successfully Tests Emergency Radio Communications 6. Amateur Radio Helps Disabled Sailboat to Port 7. Amateur Radio Takes Center Stage at The Big E Exposition In Massachusetts 8. Southern Ohio and Lawrence County Ohio Clubs To Hold Training Event 9. FCC Asks Satellite Companies For A Five Year Disposal Plan For Defunct Satellites 10. US Agency Looking At Developing Smaller Antennas For Increased Performance 11. More Hams In Africa Gain Access To The Sixty Meter Band 12. Radio Society of Great Britain Cancels Jubilee Activities On The Air Following Queens Death 13. New Entry Level Amateur Class License Is Proposed In Germany 14. Radio Amateurs of Canada Asks Hams To Comment On Proposed Callsign Changes 15. Exams For China's Class C License Have Been Rescheduled 16. New Repeater Is Dedicated To Six Broadcast Engineers Who Perished On 9-11 17. ARRL Foundation Grant Makes An Arkansas School Club Come To Life 18. 1500 Pounds Of Radio Gear Set Sail For The Upcoming Palau DxPedition 19. Radio amateurs in Ukraine diligently maintaining radio silence  20. The Starlink Incident. How a minor geomagnetic storm brough down dozens of Starlink Satellites. 21. Upcoming Contests, conventions and HamFests 22. China to hold ham radio exam session to be held in Beijing 23. Amateur radio makes the connection to save lives in Wisconsin and Idaho 24. New Zealand resident fined for importing hand held transceivers without a license or permit 25. Ofcom to release 700 MHz spectrum for PSNI/Police Service of Northern Ireland 26. RSGB announcements following the death of Her Majesty Queen Elizabeth II 27. International Amateur Radio Union Region One monthly HF bands interference report Plus these Special Features This Week: * Technology News and Commentary with Leo Laporte, W6TWT. We jump back in time to August of 2021 with a Best of The Tech Guy as Leo wishes a Happy 40th Birthday to the original IBM PC, with a retrospective look back.   * Working Amateur Radio Satellites with Bruce Paige, KK5DO - AMSAT Satellite News * Tower Climbing and Antenna Safety w/Greg Stoddard  KF9MP, looks at tower mounted electronics with a look at his latest 900 MegaHertz installation. * Foundations of Amateur Radio with Onno Benschop VK6FLAB, continues his look at the Amateurs Code. This week it is "Being Friendly on Amateur Radio." * Weekly Propagation Forecast from the ARRL * The latest from Parks On The Air and Summits On The Air (May 2022 Report) with Vance Martin, N3VEM * Bill Continelli, W2XOY - The History of Amateur Radio. Bill returns with another edition of his summer series, Amateur Radio History Headlines. This week bill looks at the headlines that made the amateur radio news during the period between 1987 and 1991. ----- Website: https://www.twiar.net Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/twiari/ Twitter: https://www.twitter.com/twiar RSS News: https://twiar.net/?feed=rss2 iHeartRadio: https://bit.ly/iHeart-TWIAR Spotify: https://bit.ly/Spotify-TWIAR TuneIn: https://bit.ly/TuneIn-TWIAR Automated: https://twiar.net/TWIARHAM.mp3 (Static file, changed weekly) -----  Visit our website at www.twiar.net for program audio, and daily for the latest amateur radio and technology news. Air This Week in Amateur Radio on your repeater! Built in identification breaks every 10 minutes or less. This Week in Amateur Radio is heard on the air on nets and repeaters as a bulletin service all across North America, and all around the world on amateur radio repeater systems, weekends on WA0RCR on 1860 (160 Meters), and more. This Week in Amateur Radio is portable too! The bulletin/news service is available and built for air on local repeaters (check with your local clubs to see if their repeater is carrying the news service) and can be downloaded for air as a weekly podcast to your digital device from just about everywhere, including Acast, Deezer, iHeart, iTunes, Google Play, Spotify, TuneIn, Stitcher, iVoox, Blubrry, Castbox.fm, Castro, Feedburner, gPodder, Listen Notes, OverCast, Player.FM, Pandora, Podcast Gang, Podcast Republic, Podchaser, Podnova, and RSS feeds. This Week in Amateur Radio is also carried on a number of LPFM stations, so check the low power FM stations in your area. You can also stream the program to your favorite digital device by visiting our web site www.twiar.net. Or, just ask Siri, Alexa, or your Google Nest to play This Week in Amateur Radio! This Week in Amateur Radio is produced by Community Video Associates in upstate New York, and is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License. If you would like to volunteer with us as a news anchor or special segment producer please get in touch with our Executive Producer, George, via email at w2xbs77@gmail.com. Also, please feel free to follow us by joining our popular group on Facebook, and follow our feed on Twitter! Thanks to FortifiedNet.net for the server space! Thanks to Archive.org for the audio space.

title: AOGGS Star Wars Is Really....
description: Today I have two different segments for your entertainment. The first is a controversial topic amongst die-hard sci-fi fans, but I think it's been a rumor floating around for decades and maybe we can put it to bed one final time. And the second is the newest segment I am adding to the show for my 9th season. It is listener/viewer mail that I think you will enjoy. If you wish to tell me a story or two about your life growing up that you feel will benefit our listeners, I would love to read it. You can send all correspondence to the mail address mentioned in the show.  Please indicate if it is for my eyes only, or if it is ok for me to share it on the show. Unfortunately not all mail can end up being read. Tell an interesting/fun/sexy story with some details that maybe are a bit erotic, and you have a great chance of your story being read in an episode.  I hope you enjoy today's show !  My best to all of you. Big hugs. ~Joey

title: Lone Mountain Cemeteries W Woody La Bounty Oct 28 2023
description: San Francisco history guy Woody LaBounty leads a tour of the land once occupied by four big San Francisco cemeteries around Lone Mountain. Now the site of shopping centers, housing developments, and the University of San Francisco, the hills separating the Western Addition and the Richmond District were the final resting place (not! learn why not!) of more than 100,000 people from the 1850s to the early 1940s. Traipsing the edge of Laurel Hill Cemetery, Calvary Cemetery, Masonic Cemetery, the Odd Fellows Cemetery, and ending at the Columbarium.

title: TWIAR 1301 NAT
description: PODCAST: This Week in Amateur Radio Edition #1301 Release Date: February 3, 2024 Here is a summary of the news trending This Week in Amateur Radio. This week's edition is anchored by Chris Perrine, KB2FAF, Dave Wilson, WA2HOY, Will Rogers, K5WLR, Don Hulick, K2ATJ, Eric Zittel, KD2RJX, William Savacool, K2SAV, George Bowen, W2XBS, and Jessica Bowen, KC2VWX.  Produced and edited by George Bowen, W2XBS. Approximate Running Time: 2:01:00 Trending headlines in this week's bulletin service:   Podcast Download: https://archive.org/download/twiar-1301-nat/TWIAR_1301_NAT.mp3 Trending headlines in this week's bulletin service  1. AMSAT: New Portable Satellite Antenna Design is Perfect for Emergency Communications 2. AMSAT: Satellite Shorts From All Over 3. WIA: Why We Need Shortwave 2.0 4. FCC: FCC Is Giving Greater Privileges On 6 GigaHertz To Very Low Power Devices 5. FCC: FCC Updates Rules To Allow 70/80/90 Gigahertz Spectrum Bands For Ships and Aircraft 6. Morse Code Is Alive and Well at KPH 7. ARRL: The First Amateur Radio Station On The Moon, JS1YMG, Is Now Transmitting 8. ARRL: ARRL Midwest Division Convention, Winterfest 9. ARRL: Heil Sound Donates Equipment To W1AW 10. ARRL: ARRL Foundation Accepting Grant Applications In February 11. ARRL: Winter Field Day A Success For Ohio Club 12. ARRL: Errata To The 2024 Thru 2028 Amateur Extra Class Question Pool Released 13. ARRL: 160th Anniversary Of The Sinking Of The USS Housatonic 14. FCC: FCC Submits Annual Report on Illegal Radio Enforcement 15. Winter Field Day Statistics Released 16. State QSO Club Challenge Is Launched 17. Two Are Arrested For Theft Of Copper From KITX Destroyed Radio Tower 18. DLARC Announces Ham Radio and More Program Joins The Digital Library Collection 19. DxPeditioners Present A Memorial For A Friend 20. West Malaysian Amateurs Start A New Summits On The Air Group 21. Amateur Radio Education Part Of Curriculum At Staten Island School 22. RAC: Canada Strengthens Its Use It Or Lose It Spectrum Policy 23. Upcoming Contests and National Convention Listing 24. ARRL Board Approves Free Membership For Students, New Vice President Elected, and more - Part One. 25. ARRL: ARRL January Board of Directors Meeting Actions (continued) Part Two 26. ARRL: ARRL January Board of Directors Meeting Actions (continued) Part Three 27. FCC: FCC To Host Webinar Showcasing An Overview Of The FCC Experimental Licensing Program 28. ARRL: Amateur Radio Volunteers Needed For The 2024 Running Of The Boston Marathon 29. IEEE: IEEE Article Credits Amateur Radio For The Current Wireless Revolution 30. FCC Will Add Vehicles and Personnel For Enforcement Of The PIRATE Radio Act In Several Major Markets Plus these Special Features This Week: * Our technology reporter Leo Laporte, W6TWT, will discuss when is it time to buy a new router, you should look at MESS Technology, and talks about the advantages of Network Attached Storage. * Working Amateur Radio Satellites with Bruce Paige, KK5DO - AMSAT Satellite News * Foundations of Amateur Radio with Onno Benschop VK6FLAB, will answer the question "What is a repeater or a beacon, really?" * The DX Corner with Bill Salyers, AJ8B with news on DXpeditions, DX, upcoming contests and more. * Weekly Propagation Forecast from the ARRL * Bill Continelli, W2XOY - The History of Amateur Radio. This week, Bill takes us back to June 4, 1940 when the FCC issued an order that hams could not make contacts off shore, and then In December issue another order that took the amateurs totally off the air. * RAIN/QSO TODAY: We will conclude our two part interview with Gordon West, WB6NOA, and how he began his amateur radio journey. ----- Website: https://www.twiar.net X: @twiar Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/twiari RSS News: https://twiar.net/?feed=rss2 Automated: https://twiar.net/TWIARHAM.mp3 (Static file, changed weekly) -----  Visit our website at www.twiar.net for program audio, and daily for the latest amateur radio and technology news. You can air This Week in Amateur Radio on your repeater! Built in identification breaks every 10 minutes or less. This Week in Amateur Radio is heard on the air on nets and repeaters as a bulletin service all across North America, and all around the world on amateur radio repeater systems, weekends on WA0RCR on 1860 (160 Meters), and more. This Week in Amateur Radio is portable too! The bulletin/news service is available and built for air on local repeaters (check with your local clubs to see if their repeater is carrying the news service) and can be downloaded for air as a weekly podcast to your digital device from just about everywhere. This Week in Amateur Radio is also carried on a number of LPFM stations, so check the low power FM stations in your area. You can also stream the program to your favorite digital device by visiting our web site www.twiar.net. Or, just ask Siri, Alexa, or your Google Nest to play This Week in Amateur Radio! This Week in Amateur Radio is produced by Community Video Associates in upstate New York, and is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License. If you would like to volunteer with us as a news anchor or special segment producer please get in touch with our Executive Producer, George, via email at w2xbs77@gmail.com. Also, please feel free to follow us by joining our popular group on Facebook, and follow our feed on X! Thanks to FortifiedNet.net for the server space! Thanks to Archive.org for the audio space.

With us, you will face demons that lie in wait and experience nightmares that shall leave you sleepless. Do you have the courage to enter our circle? If not, will you bow to our threats? We think you will… Today, we discuss Clive Barker's Hellraiser. Hosts: Follow Daniel @Dstarsick on Twitter Follow Ryan @DarthDaimyo on Twitter Find the podcast on Twitter @HorrorPod69. title: SR218 - Monkeeastronaut : "The Jungle Within"
description: https://monkeeastronaut.bandcamp.com/ Monkeeastronaut is a solo project of the Italian musician Riccardo Occhionero. The purpose is to bring an experimental mixture of soundscapes trying to evoke for the listener a sense of bewilderment, fear and astonishment, just like a monkey could feel travelling through space. Basically it's an attempt to explore how human curiosity and arrogance can generate forms of evil that are against the nature of things, using innocent living forms as oblivious witnesses of the utopistic reach of a knowledge that is far beyond the present step in evolution. Oh and there's some random improvisation as well.  "Beware the beast Man, for he is the Devil's pawn. Alone among God's primates, he kills for sport or lust or greed. Yea, he will murder his brother to possess his brother's land. Let him not breed in great numbers, for he will make a desert of his home and yours. Shun him; drive him back into his jungle lair, for he is the harbinger of death."   (Planet Of The Apes - 1968)

This year’s event celebrated the 13th anniversary of the City’s  transformative open data law  and concluded  NYC Open Data Week 2025 . NYC School of Data is a community driven conference with a focus on open data, civic technology, and service design. The main stage was webcast via  ISOC.LIVE .  ASL interpretation will be provided . Other tracks were recorded and made available via the BetaNYC YouTube channel. Much appreciation to  Reinvent Albany  for the funding support. title: AOGGS - Summer Talk 1 - Being Old, Feeling Young
description: In this last episode of the show before ' Sweeps Period 2023 ' !!, I give you a discussion (amongst myself) of several topics.  - What to expect from this year's  Sweeps Period - Is Box Dancing at clubs again in my future? - Why am I a bit like Blanche Devereaux ? - What was it like being General Manager of a gym,  - Where was my prominent position at Provincetown's BIG TEA DANCE? - What is the story behind my shaving my head (I was a pioneer !)? - Early thoughts about my husband Paco and I buying our first house.  This is a big deal. I've lived in my apartment for 25 years ! I thought I'd die here LOL ! But...Nope ! - and a few jokes along the way to make you smile. SWEEPS PERIOD 2023  begins this Saturday, July 1st. It will be almost all STORYTIME episodes during this year's  SWEEPS ! Please SUBSCRIBE and join me ! ~Joey

title: The Bottled City (D&D - Adventure)
description: If you´d like to support Patrick Hoffmann, then you may click this link to purchase this piece for a price, you'd deem fair. Long ago there lived a thief by the name of Kazeem Alhir. Known as the greatest thief of his time, during his life of high adventure Kazeem found a magic ring deep in the earth. This ring allowed him to control a mighty Djinni, and in turn have his every wish granted. Using the Djinni, Kazeem overthrew the tyrants controlling his homeland and created a prosperous kingdom. Though great and powerful, Kazeem feared for his fortune in death and with the help of the ring, he created a bottled city to seal himself and his fortune inside, never to be found. Until now... Find within a sprawling urban quest that brings a whole new meaning to the classic "sandbox" gameplay! A one-shot 5th edition Dungeons and Dragons adventure optimized for 3rd - 5th level characters with monsters, maps, and more. Approximately 4 - 6 hours of play.

title: "Fiasco"
description: Read at Your Own Risk   Before reading Stanislaw Lem’s “FIASCO” you might consider doing a modicum of homework. Plus, strap yourself into a sturdy chair (or other device as might be required for acceleration to light speed) since you will be challenged in some of your most cherished illusions – specifically, “What am I?” and “What do I know about the world I live in beyond the few millimeters from the end of my nose?”   Some topics you must select for (in depth) study might be: Higher Order Mathematics including Probability Calculus, Cosmogony and Cosmology, Futurology, Philosophy of Science, Mysticism, and the included fields of Inorganic and Organic Chemistry (at low kinetic energies), Vulcanism and Tectonics, Atomic Transmutations, Forms of Radiation included in the Electromagnetic Spectrum and otherwise, Logic, Relativity, Physics of Newton and Einstein, Information Theory, Thermonuclear Functions, Darwinian Evolution, the Biochemistry of Living Systems, Psychology, History of Technology, Game Theory and everything else an Informed Intelligence of the 21th Century Should Know. Further, if you wanted to live a life of “Understanding” what types of experts might you align yourself with in your quests as in some kind of Think Tank? Here the personalities of the Faceless Experts will consist entirely of their Fields of Study. Then consider how would you adjust yourself to a seemingly conscious Artificial Intelligence device you must also communicate with? Just to be sure, when all the available alternatives fail, is there anything else?   Do not expect Stan to be of much help in clarifying these matters, since he will assume you know just about all of them as well as his perfect memory. “FIASCO” is a rather odd example of what is called “Hard Science Fiction” not because it is difficult, but because the emphasis is on the Science, and where the fiction begins is up to the reader to determine. Relatedly, where might Stan’s knowledge, imagination and understandings become entangled or incomplete? Does Stan know enough about liquid crystals and the nature of quasi-static systems? Or, what role does teleology play in chemical changes rather than opportunism? Or, how do new genes of the Orphan variety actually arise? Are gravitational and inertial masses really different? How is identity best determined? The reader must decide. Ideas developed after l987 are not mentioned, including the analysis of the Genome. Curiously, Stan exhibits considerable respect for the mystical science fiction writer, Phillip K. Dick who was without advanced formal education beyond the contents of the encyclopedias Britannica and Philosophy. Lem was educated at two universities, one including medical training.   In a recent issue of The New Scientist its yearend lead story tries to make predictions regarding future developments. That is, if we should contact alien life, reengineer life, cause pandemics, nuclear wars, end population growth and the like, what would happen? Most science fiction writers try to do the same by extrapolating from present trends into the future, and usually get things wrong. The article is less critical of Dick and Lem, who invent entirely new futures in considerable detail with nonlinear extrapolations. New planets are being discovered with characteristics similar to or completely different from the Earth.   Would life be predictable on these worlds, or is the anthropocentric view always a disabling limitation? Lem and Dick attempt to make informative predictions of future developments. In contrast to Dick who became enamored with direct communication with the Universe through mystical revelations, Lem uses the results of his philosophical reasoning and documented facts. Some questions entertained are - What are the interactions of technology and the biosphere? Could elaborate mechanisms replace the biogeochemical cycles? What are the types of intelligence? What are the stages in societal development? Would advanced societies advertise or conceal their existence? Could life systems based on elements other than carbon be recognizable at all? That is, is there any requirement in physical structure necessary to develop a psyche?   How would competing societies coexist? Could humans investigate worlds separated by interstellar distances by traveling at near light velocities? What would be the structural and functional details of vehicles capable of making such trips?            The Literature Fan, and even the Science Fan might take a rather lame approach to the science in “FIASCO,” and speed read through Stan’s chapter long expositions. Rather like Dostoyevsky, Lem thinks that everyone met on the way down the street is wondering about the latest developments in all fields of study, and is willing to listen to a friend’s (is there such a thing?) hour long (or longer) exposition on the most esoteric of subjects. If you skip the science of “FIASCO” there is not much left - the characters in the novel do not participate in what could be called “interpersonal relationships” – “How are you today?” Nobody cares!   Now, one caveat: The interpersonal relationships which are included in the plot involve the protocols to be followed in dealing with completely alien civilizations, and the determination if any such protocols exist (or could exist) for communication with or even recognition of an alien life developed light years from the Earth. Here, Stan takes aim at the goals of SETI (Search for Extra Terrestrial Intelligence) and CETI (Communication with Extra Terrestrial Intelligence) organizations. Such goals might be the basis of the name, “FIASCO” chosen for the novel as a whole. Some of Stan’s other writings are of the philosophical variety – and here care must be taken to avoid blending personal biases with profound factual knowledge.   To achieve his far-reaching goals Stan begins with an intrepid fellow searching for a lost team of workers in the weirdly transformative landscape of the volcanically active Titan,   the great moon of Saturn. His transport vehicle is described in some detail, and effective as it is, it finally fails and the rescuer is killed. Subsequently, he is found and revived. This same fellow is then chosen (one hundred years later!!) to be part of a crew on an interstellar exploratory vehicle to visit a distant solar system where life might exist. The details of his reanimation and the structure of the vehicle are described again in considerable detail. How did the fellow’s brain react to the freezing temperatures of Titan? Will he recover his lost Illusion of Personal Identity, or does it even matter? What structure must the vehicle have to maintain its passengers in a type of embryonic state to withstand the time and stresses of acceleration to velocities necessary for the journey? The destination is finally reached and the crew begins the study of the planet which is revealed as “curiouser and curiouser.” One of the curiosities among the many is that a portion of the world ocean seems to have been pumped into the upper reaches of the atmosphere possibly to reveal larger tracts of inhabitable land. Even more curious are the seeming “life forms” and their attempts to hide, reveal or protect themselves from the visitors they seem to have detected. Initially, a thorough search of the terrain yields no (recognizable) evidence of the inhabitants (skeletons, etc.) in the planet surface. Technological “structures” are limited to arrangements of repetitive geometric forms. The strategies the humans have decided on (including a probe to the planet’s surface) are developed with the help of a supreme artificial intelligence device whose thoughts are always revealed as superior logic, but lacking in emotion. As the interaction of the life forms begins the humans find in most cases that their attempts at communication are less than prudent. How to avoid being seen as “intruders” who invite retaliation in the form of certain metal transforming “viruses”?   In developing the plot of FIASCO, Stan takes an approach very similar to Charles Dodgson in “Alice in Wonderland” and “Through the Looking Glass.” Names and places in both novels are the changeable, “Who’s?” and so are the rather uncertain, “Where’s?” Significant scenes and characters are Alice and Angus Purvis both of whom have forgotten their personal identities as discussed in the Caterpillar incident where Alice admits doubts about who she is, and the location of the “where” to go; thus the destination falls into question in Alice’s discussion with the Cheshire Cat – “Go in any direction you will end up somewhere.” “Who are you?” and “Where are you going?” are central issues. In addition, the “How?” of finding out is quite odd as Alice discovers as she attends the tea party where the Mad Hatter examines his faulty watch (time) whose function is not improved by butter with crumbs (faulty conception of problem solving), and the seeming unlimited number of tea settings about the table, an infinity leading to the original starting point (and then vertically?). Movements as in the jumps of chess pieces are routine. The Rabbit Hole to Wonderland is quite suggestive of the distortions of space and time experienced at near light velocity. Finally, the novel might be a riddle the solution for which no one “has the slightest idea”.   Therefore, the reader might need a scorecard to keep track of the location and the destination of Stan’s crew is trying to reach. Here is one attempt to clarify the matter. The “Where?” is located in the constellation “Harpy” many light years from the Earth, in which is an invisible, solitary collapsar in Beta harpiae. Though invisible the star reveals itself by causing perturbations in the movements of the nearby visible stars, Alpha, Gamma and Delta. The specific name of the collapsar is Hades (Don’t go too near it!!). This energy-depleted system is seemingly attached to another Universe. The mission’s actual destination is the planet, “Quinta,” the sixth planet of the sixth sun of Harpy. Unique features of Quinta become immediately known by its multitudes of radiations. The Quinta crew is provided with vessels named Eurydice, Orpheus and Hermes, and landers like the Gabriel, some manned and some not. Sources of fuel for the vehicles are numerous including the rarefied gases collected from space during travel to energy derived from the artificially induced vibrations in the collapsar.   The crew is composed of numerous specialized individuals, including Angus Parvis (also known as Mark, Tempe, and Tel Horabe) (second pilot); Arago (Dominican monk who provides ideas based on faith); Lauger (physicist); Gold (astrophysicists, sociologist); Nakamura (astrophysicist); Gerbert (physician); Steergard (captain of Hermes); Gerbert (reconnaissance crew) Polassar (also reconnaissance crew); Harrach (first pilot of the Hermes); Ramont (logician); Kinsting and El Salam (exobiologists). And, needless to say there are undoubtedly others sprinkled here and there by Stan and left for the readers to discover. Finally, the essential members of the crew are multiple examples of sentient computers all referred to as Deus (they consider themselves “cousins”).   A problem posed for the crew by Stan is that they must contact the aliens of Quinta even before they want to reveal themselves. On the destruction of a probe, the members of the crew hold a conference on what to do next. Tangentially, it is as though a “robocaller” is attempting to entice a contact to come in to buy a car. How to avoid offending him, present yourself as interesting and sympathetic, leave the fellow no alternative and in the end to achieve an appointment? One might mention the envy associates could develop when a car is obtained under “enviable” arrangements. Also, paying the bill might bring about the exhaustion of the subject’s resources. Of course, there is one alternative however unlikely - the prospect being harmed if he declines, somewhat in the manner of the Godfather, “Making an offer you can’t refuse”. Sending down friendly bottles of Brandy from ship stores is never brought up.   The crew members also try to deduce the nature of possible conflicts raging over time on Quinta. The scenarios are impossibly complex since the existence of the aliens has not been confirmed. One such extreme idea is that the alien factions no longer confront one another, and a war rages based on the devices of the weapons alone. That there may be no war at all is another unrecognized alternative. The crew members might have the similar problem revealed in the story, “The Emperor’s New Clothes.” Where is the Little Boy with a fresh vision when you need one?   As the reader of FIASCO progresses through the novel an undeniable inspiration is suggested by the story, “Farewell to the Master” by Harry Bates the first editor of Astounding Stories magazine, in which the story published in 1940. Also indicated here is the film adaptation, “The Day the World Stood Still” from 195l, the screenplay by Edmund H. North. In the film an alien, Klaatu comes to Earth for an encounter to warn the population that if humans send rockets into space and in the process spreads the culture of petty jealousies and stupidity across the Cosmos they invite their own destruction. An illustrative event involves a worldwide loss of electrical power. Unfortunately, the film also includes religious connotations to limit the power of the companion robot Gort, and the “J.C” initials of the Klaatu pseudonym, John Carpenter. Critics regret the sense of wonder from the original story is largely lost. (Just possibly, a science fiction story mentioned in FIASCO,” is the Bates story identified above).   In the most general form (here even to the degree of seeming initially a digression) the recognition of life depends on its distinctive characteristics. Current knowledge suggests a living entity achieves existence worthiness either by “durability” (a mountain) or “replicability” (a raindrop). Living things replicate more rapidly than entropy destroys them. To achieve this end they take advantage of environmental substances, including other life forms. One of the most highly developed life forms in these respects are the worms known as “tardigrades.” They are not only skilled replicators but also able to withstand wide extremes of environmental conditions including those of outer space. Ultimately, replication is an expression of a set of molecules within them, the nucleic acids (DNA and RNA) unique in nature in their ability to self-replicate. Other components of the organism facilitate this autocatalytic procedure as the basis of which is the processing of information stored in the nucleic acids regarding ordering of the environment. That order originally derived from association of simple cells with the reducing environment of oceanic “fumaroles.” Oddly, Lem does not mention the principles of “Panspermia.” Frequently, life forms join in consortiums ranging from predation and proto cooperation to mutualism. Humans are particularly skilled in such associations, largely due to being adept in procedures including Ingenuity, Audacity and Originality, and   others including “Stupidity.”   Natural communal animal systems are introduced in “FIASCO” in an extensive review of South American termites. Here, “We” is an illusion developed when a group of separate entities become a unified whole - analogous to the arrangement of cells in a multicellular organism maintained by the immune system. No mention is made in “FIASCO” of the consciousness of atoms and molecules as has been suggested in Quantum Physics, although one investigator is searching that quality based on the quantum states of calcium phosphate. Intelligence is minimally defined as the capacity to develop knowledge and apply skills. Stan suggests the unseen Quintans certainly do have intelligence.            At the gross societal level, precedents of communication will also come to mind, some judged “fraudulent” and others relatively “harmless”.   Edgar Allan Poe in his short story, “Diddling” makes reference to the first variety for taking advantage of a stranger for personal gain. This instance involves spontaneously offering drugs to strangers on a street corner, taking their money, promising to obtain and return with the purchase, and then simply disappearing. An example of the less virulent type involves an American physicist who communicated with the president about the possibility of the Atomic Bomb.   He later regretted sending the communication when he saw the results. In the actual scenario, the enemy was warned, a single bomb dropped, and then a second when surrender was not achieved.   Similarly, at the outbreak of World War I the assassination of Archduke Ferdinand precipitated spontaneous alliances among Germany, Austria, Great Britain, Russia and Serbia to avoid hostilities until, “The generals got back from vacation.”   Further, Great Britain offered asylum to the Romanov family only to withdraw it for fear of spoiling public opinion. In the scenarios in “FIASCO,” science, logic and all the disciplines mentioned at the outset tell authorities in a civilization “What they can do.” Oddly, the same physicist already mentioned suggested that the best ideas are formed wholly in the human mind without reference to observable objects. Where does the counsel of such authority tell humans as he once queried, “What they should do?” “Flipping a coin” as a suggested in Game Theory when no alternative of action can be decided on, seems of doubtful effectiveness although coercive when no alternatives exist. In “FIASCO” the humans are very intent on meeting with the “intelligent” Quintans person to person although they might remain reticent. What would they be like? Evidence is available to suggest Slime Molds are minimally intelligent, as expressed by the capacity to determine the shortest path to a food source through a maze. Yet, at the gross morphological level they are quite unlike humans. Pigeons are intelligent enough to develop superstitions. How different are human sciences from a set of complex superstitions? In a cage, food is provided at irregular intervals and the birds tend to repeat behaviors performed just before the last feeding. Forest trees show evidence of communicating with other trees through root systems that loggers are cutting at them. Plants also become disoriented when their position relative to lighting or one-sided force of wind is altered. Of course, such effects are all based on chemistry and physics. Yet, the psyche influences the chemistry of a human and chemistry influences the psyche.   This dichotomy is almost certainly an illusion.       Humans and aliens might have developed a psyche based on completely different Jungian archetypal influences over their evolutionary history. They might have completely different or no equivalent concepts of “trust.” or “honesty”? Further, the archetypal analytical concepts of modern physics all (or nearly all) are to be found originally in the philosophy of ancient Greece.   Where would they originate on a planet with quite different form and function? In several scenes the Humans express a kind of malaise in the repetitive world of the spacecraft, especially that brought on by the seeming hopelessness of the enterprise.   One of them exhibits Romantic feelings inspired by the structure of the shrubbery in the greenhouse of Eurydice, emotions remarkably similar to the expressed by Wordsworth in his, “Prelude.” How general is Romanticism among organisms? Elephants have been observed transfixed by the setting sun over the African veldt. If all natural ecosystems were replaced by technology, would a cockroach inspire poetry by creeping down the wall of a spacecraft cabin?   Finally, when communicating with aliens a necessary constraint might be variation in explanation of the connectedness of events. In Humans of the western world, “causality” is the dominant explanation. However, in the eastern world such connectedness is attributed to “Pre Established Harmony”. If all human language and conception is based on causality and the alien on Pre Established Harmony as their only possible way of thinking, communication is not possible.   In conclusion, FIASCO is considered Stan’s “Crowning Achievement.” Admittedly, in his considerations of all these topics and more Stan’s erudition is impressive especially with respect to forms of communication and the radiation and particular forms of technology to apply them. The disinterested reader might find his attention waxing and waning during the lengthy expositions. However, such expositions form the Science Fiction as apposed to Sci. Fi.   On the contrary, one of the great pleasures of life is to examine personal beliefs and to alter them when substantive evidence appears. One definition of Science is, “Perpetual Doubt.” Further, this same disinterested reader might develop a compulsion to continue reading in spite of flagging enthusiasm to erase ignorance of clever puzzles and their solutions. One such puzzle presented by the Universe is that it hides the solutions to its most tantalizing features. Scientists, mathematicians, and other thinkers clamor to find a solution to clever puzzles, including “Why is a raven like a writing desk?” In the number system invented by humans why do “prime numbers appear”? Why do scientists search for life in the Universe? One cosmologist has admitted, “So that I am not alone!” Another admitted he studied the structure of the Universe because he wanted it to be beautiful, that beauty expressed in the elegance of Mathematics. Why is it that Relativity and Quantum Physics can be made compatible to a millionth, billionth, billionth, billionth of a centimeter, and then chaos develops?   No comment can be made here on Stan’s writing style because “FIASCO” will be read as a translation. However, a suspicion might develop in the reader’s mind, that the story is incidental to a philosophic presentation of certain of Stan’s mind meanderings. The same reference could be made to Dostoyevsky when he presents in “The Brothers Karamazov” the fifty-page summary of the prosecutor’s case at the trial. Dostoyevsky admitted when he reached one thousand pages of the story he said everything he wanted to say.   On the final page of “FIASCO” Stan reveals the Quintans. Is this revelation a satisfactory ending of exceptional imagination, or a failure of imagination? Is the reader informed or disappointed? Do the characters in the novel live, “happily ever after.” or are they depicted as participating in a “fiasco” where the enterprise yielded nothing but the thought, “Maybe we should have done something else!”   The reader’s reaction will rely on past life experiences, and the human tendency to feel intellectually inferior when “long, hard books” cannot be read and understood.   A recent educational “expert” says there is no such thing as “understanding.” Leonard Zelig would have been pleased when he did not understand, “Moby Dick” after reading that long, hard book. Anyway, order a copy of “FIASCO” from Amazon. Read it several times. You will find yourself reflecting on the ideas and puzzles, between readings and long after the final reading is completed.     Summarized above are some of the reflections of one reader. Apologies are provided if the remarks are wide of the mark. But, like a fairy tale Stanislaw’s novel might be improved with each variant telling.     Your Old Buddy, "Whizbang!!"

title: Episode 89 | The Exorcist (1973)

Join us as we delve into the depths of literature’s most warped minds: King, Stoker, Shelley, Ito, Matheson, Koontz, Jackson, and many more. With us, you will face demons that lie in wait and experience nightmares that shall leave you sleepless. Do you have the courage to enter our circle? If not, will you bow to our threats? title: World Health Organization: How to Wear a Mask (PSA)
description: The World Health Organization demonstrates the proper techniques for wearing a mask or face covering. Disposable masks and face coverings should be thrown away after use. Reusable face coverings should be washed after use, and can be reused again once clean. The CDC recommends that the general public should wear non-medical grade face coverings whenever venturing outside to take part in essential activities. The City of Cupertino would like to express thanks to the World Health Organization for permission to use their video materials during COVID-19 pandemic. More information can be found at https://who.int/covid-19 For more information regarding the impact of the COVID-19 outbreak in Cupertino, please visit https://cupertino.org/coronavirus

title: TWIAR 1303 NAT
description: PODCAST: This Week in Amateur Radio Edition #1303 - Full Version Release Date: February 17, 2024 Here is a summary of the news trending This Week in Amateur Radio. This week's edition is anchored by Chris Perrine, KB2FAF, Denny Haight, NZ8D, Don Hulick, K2ATJ, Dave Wilson, WA2HOY, Will Rogers, K5WLR, Marvin Turner, W0MET, Eric Zittel, KD2RJX, Bob Donlon. W3BOO, George Bowen, W2XBS, and Jessica Bowen, KC2VWX.  Produced and edited by George Bowen, W2XBS. Approximate Running Time: 2:03:56 Trending headlines in this week's bulletin service:   Podcast Download: https://bit.ly/TWIAR1303 Trending headlines in this week's bulletin service  1. AMSAT: GreenCube IO-117 Continues Operations Beyond Expected February 5th Passivation 2. AMSAT: JS1YMG: Decoding The First Moon Based Ham Radio Station’s Telemetry Signals 3. AMSAT: Satellite Shorts From All Over 4. FCC: The FCC Bans Robocalls With AI Generated Voices  5. Earth Has Received Power Beamed From A Satellite In Space For The First Time 6. ARRL: Senators Wicker & Blumenthal Introduce S.3690 To Eliminate Private Land Use Restrictions on AR 7. RAC: Barrie Crampton, VE3BSB, appointed to Canadian Amateur Radio Hall of Fame 8. ARRL: Amateur Radio As An Educational Tool Represented To Policymakers In Washington DC 9. ARRL: Highlights From The 2024 Orlando HamCation 10. ARRL: Veteran DXpedition Leader Bob Allphin, K4UEE, SK 11. ARRL: Registration Deadline For The HamSCI Sixth Annual Workshop Is March 1st 12. ARRL: Errata to the 2024 - 2028 Amateur Extra-Class Question Pool Released (Updated) 13. WIA: Hams In Australia Have New License Procedures Taking Effect 14. European Space Agency Proposes New Ham Satellite Payload, Seeks Comments 15. Amateur Antennas Would Be Protected Under Proposed New Law In South Carolina 16. DMR TGIF Founder EA7KDO, Mitch Savage, SK 17. L3 Harris Technologies Prototype Antenna System and Satellites Receive High Marks 18. Jose Jacob VU2JOS Receives Lifetime Award 19. Hawaii Amateurs To Develop Emergency Simplex Radio Net 20. First Responders In West Virginia Organize A New Ham Radio Club 21. FCC: FCC Moves To Add 13 New Languages To Emergency Alert System On Radio 22. ARRL: ARRL Board of Directors Meets - Headquarters To Close on Presidents Day 23. Upcoming contests, conventions and hamfests 24. ARRL: Amateur Radio goes to high school in New York City  25. ARRL: George Washington's Birthday will be celebrated by three separate radio clubs 26. ARRL: The DX Mentor Recognition Program is announced 27. RSGB: Amateur radio license changes begin this month in the United Kingdom 28. ARRL: Monthly Volunteer Monitoring Program Report. Plus these Special Features This Week: * Our technology reporter Leo Laporte, W6TWT, takes a look at why you really don't need certain types of software on your computer.  * Working Amateur Radio Satellites with Bruce Paige, KK5DO - AMSAT Satellite News * Foundations of Amateur Radio with Onno Benschop VK6FLAB, answers the question, "What is an Amateur Radio Emergency Response?"  * The DX Corner with Bill Salyers, AJ8B with news on DXpeditions, DX, upcoming contests and more. * Weekly Propagation Forecast from the ARRL * Bill Continelli, W2XOY - The History of Amateur Radio. This week, Bill takes us in The Wayback Machine to December 7th, 1941 during World War Two when the FCC issued Order 87 which shuts down amateur radio in the US, and creates, WERS, the War Emergency Service. * RAIN: The Rain Report we will hear an interview with Alex Muzyka, the man and mind behind the his KQ2H 10 Meter FM repeater in the Catskill mountains of New York. ----- Website: https://www.twiar.net X: @twiar Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/twiari RSS News: https://twiar.net/?feed=rss2 Automated (Full): https://twiar.net/TWIARHAM.mp3 (Static file, updated weekly) Automated (1-hour): https://www.twiar.net/TWIAR1HR.mp3 (Static file, updated weekly) -----  Visit our website at www.twiar.net for program audio, and daily for the latest amateur radio and technology news. You can air This Week in Amateur Radio on your repeater! Built in identification breaks every 10 minutes or less. This Week in Amateur Radio is heard on the air on nets and repeaters as a bulletin service all across North America, and all around the world on amateur radio repeater systems, weekends on WA0RCR on 1860 (160 Meters), and more. This Week in Amateur Radio is portable too! The bulletin/news service is available and built for air on local repeaters (check with your local clubs to see if their repeater is carrying the news service) and can be downloaded for air as a weekly podcast to your digital device from just about everywhere. This Week in Amateur Radio is also carried on a number of LPFM stations, so check the low power FM stations in your area. You can also stream the program to your favorite digital device by visiting our web site www.twiar.net. Or, just ask Siri, Alexa, or your Google Nest to play This Week in Amateur Radio! This Week in Amateur Radio is produced by Community Video Associates in upstate New York, and is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License. title: 🇪🇸 Hot day at Barcelona beach
description: Until the end of the twentieth century, the beaches in Barcelona were not very appealing. It was only in the 90’s that the various beaches were transformed, cleaning the water and the sand to make them more attractive for locals and tourists to take a dip. Although Barcelona is not considered to be a beach holiday destination, millions of people go to the city’s numerous beaches to sunbathe and swim in the Mediterranean Sea every year. A promenade runs along all the city’s coastline and crosses every beach, from LLevant (northern side of the city) to Sant Sebastià (southern part of Barcelona), where the renowned Hotel W is. All the beaches in Barcelona are well connected, maintained and equipped with all the services one would imagine a beach to have. The closest to the city center are La Barceloneta, Sant Miquel and Sant Sebastià. I hope you enjoy my videos from these beautiful beaches. All the videos are created and edited by Beach4U in 4K resolution without any effects and color filters. If you like my videos please Subscribe my channel and hit the Like button and notification bell for support .! Music: YouTube Audio library It's pleasure to hear about your favorite Beaches you like to see, so please feel free to ask and comment them below the videos. Thanks for watching and look forward to your feedback! WARNING : It's illegal to copy and use any of my videos without permission. *Copyright © 2023 Beach4U. All Rights Reserved.

title: Why I Garden This Way Every Day | World Naked Gardening Day
description: In this video, I share why I garden all natural and what drew me to gardening. I believe that our connection to nature is vital for our health, and for the health of the planet. That's why I soak up the sun and let the earth touch my skin as often as possible. ::: PATREON https://www.patreon.com/nakedgardener ::: THE NAKED GARDENER HERBAL TEA BLENDS https://www.thenakedgardener.us/store ::: BAREBONES GARDEN TOOLS https://alnk.to/aLDcMIe ::: JACKERY SOLAR & POWER https://shrsl.com/3cxhf Sharing my story about moving from the desert in Arizona to a secluded part of land in the Midwest to build a permaculture inspired garden and thriving homestead. Aside from a few raised garden beds in the past, I have never done anything like this before on this scale. I'm learning as I go along, but am sharing my love and enthusiasm for reconnecting with nature, growing my own food, and living more sustainably. I believe that our connection to nature is vital for our health, and for the health of the planet. We must come back to the earth to sustain life and live more vibrantly, as nature intended. I garden the NAKED way. I don't use any herbicides, pesticides, or chemicals, and that carries through in every other aspect of my life as well - from the type of products I use, the food I eat, and the environment I keep. I also soak up the sun and let the earth touch my skin as often as possible ;) Thank you for watching! Please support my channel by liking and subscribing, or by joining me on Patreon. Music from various artists including: DC Schroeder ...................................... WEBSITE: https://www.thenakedgardener.us/ PATREON: https://www.patreon.com/nakedgardener INSTAGRAM: thenakedgardener_official RUMBLE: https://rumble.com/c/thenakedgardener #homesteading​ #organicgardening #foodforest

title: Trying To Understand Multiverses 2020
description: Trying To Understand Multiverses 2020 A kind of complex mixed explanation of the Multiverse as we/some of (us) understand it in the year of "June/2020" and the difference "between" Time Travel and the Multiverse The  multiverse  is a hypothetical group of multiple universes. Together, these universes comprise everything that exists: the entirety of space, time, matter, energy, information, and the physical laws and constants that describe them.

title: TWIAR 1302 NAT
description: PODCAST: This Week in Amateur Radio Edition #1302 Release Date: February 10, 2024 Here is a summary of the news trending This Week in Amateur Radio. This week's edition is anchored by Denny Haight, NZ8D, Dave Wilson, WA2HOY, Don Hulick, K2ATJ, Will Rogers, K5WLR, Rich Lawrence, KB2MOB, Eric Zittel, KD2RJX Bob Donlon, W3BOO, George Bowen, W2XBS, and Jessica Bowen, KC2VWX.  Produced and edited by George Bowen, W2XBS. Approximate Running Time: 1:26:24 Trending headlines in this week's bulletin service:   Podcast Download: https://bit.ly/TWIAR1302 Trending headlines in this week's bulletin service  1. AMSAT: AO-92 Re-enters Earth’s Atmosphere 2. AMSAT: Lunar Excursion Vehicle (LEV–1) Amateur Telemetry Received 3. WIA: New Indian Documentary Is A Collection Of Nostalgic Stories Of Radio Enthusiasts 4. Plan For Europe's Huge New Particle Collider Takes Shape 5. An Entire 200 Foot Tower Stolen From WJLX-AM Jasper Alabama 6. ARRL: California Storms: Amateur Radio Is Ready 7. ARRL: Amateur Radio Goes To High School In New York City 8. ARRL: World Radio Day 2024 9. ARRL: George Washington's Birthday Will Be Celebrated By Three Amateur Radio Clubs 10. ARRL: Georgia Elementary School Contacts The International Space Station 11. ARRL: DX Mentor Recognition Program Is Announced 12. Developing Nations CubeSats Are Getting A Ride To Orbit 13. Amateur Radio License Changes Begin This Month In The United Kingdom 14. Contester Slavko Celarc, S57DX, A Silent Key 15. International YL Club Plans A Czech Contest Station 16. Pluto's Discovery Celebrated On The Amateur Bands 17. Silicon Valley Explores Its Amateur Radio Past 18. A Major Festival In North Bengal Supported By Amateurs 19. WIA: Amelia Earhart's Long Lost Plane Possibly Detected By Sonar 20. Upcoming Select Contests and Conventions 21. Monthly Volunteer Monitoring System Report 22. ARRL: The first amateur radio signal originating on the moon JS1YMG is on the air 23. ARRL: Heil Sound makes a large donation to the W1AW stations 24. ARRL: ARRL Foundation is now accepting grant applications  25. ARRL: Volunteer Examiner Coordinators issues an Errata to the 2024-2028 Amateur Extra Class Question Pool 26. FCC: FCC submits its annual report on illegal radio enforcement to Congress 27. DLARC: The DLARC announces that Ham Radio & More program is added to its digital library 28. RAC: Canada strengthens its use it or lose it spectrum policy Plus these Special Features This Week: * Our technology reporter Leo Laporte, W6TWT talks about how computers are getting better, but how keyboards are getting worse. * Working Amateur Radio Satellites with Bruce Paige, KK5DO - AMSAT Satellite News * Foundations of Amateur Radio with Onno Benschop VK6FLAB, will answer the question "What is the right mode for Emergency Communications?' * The DX Corner with Bill Salyers, AJ8B with news on DXpeditions, DX, upcoming contests and more. * Weekly Propagation Forecast from the ARRL * Will Rogers, K5WLR, our amateur radio historian, will take the wayback machine to December of 1915 to take a look at the first edition of a little magazine called QST. ----- Website: https://www.twiar.net X: @twiar Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/twiari RSS News: https://twiar.net/?feed=rss2 Automated: https://twiar.net/TWIARHAM.mp3 (Static file, changed weekly) -----  Visit our website at www.twiar.net for program audio, and daily for the latest amateur radio and technology news. title: Flash Traffic - City Of Angels (Windows 10 Compatible)
description: UPDATED!! Fixed and repackaged installer... apologies for anyone who downloaded the first version... Here's another old DOS game made compatible to run in Windows 10... from Time Warner Interactive "Flash Traffic - City Of Angels" Silent installer, copies all the necessary files and runs through DOSBox emulator... compatible with Windows 7 through to 10, XP users this installer will not work! :P Plot: (From wikipedia.org) The DEA  hit a warehouse, where they kill two people and take three suspects. They believe the warehouse only contains drugs, but find the materials to make a  n uclear bomb . The player is tasked to find out who made the bomb, retrieve it, and defuse it before it destroys the Northern Hempsphere. A Time Warner Interactive / White Bob Installer Presentation... Have fun! :)

title: A visit to the abandoned Boonton Line (Greenwood Lake Branch) around Branch Brook Park
description: These are a few pictures from the Boonton Branch around the Branch Brook Park area before purchase and conversion into a rail trail. This section of the Boonton Line connected Montclair and Hoboken. Passenger service ended on the Boonton Line on September 20th, 2002 with the opening of the Montclair connection, giving direct access from Montclair to the Northeast Corridor to New York City or the Morris and Essex Line to Hoboken. Freight service (to the Orange Branch) lasted through 2010, sharing the right-of-way with the Newark Light Rail extension. Advocates had sought for decades the conversion of the abandoned right-of-way to a rail trail. In November 2021, the process for acquiring the right-of-way had formally begun. On September 15th, 2022, Governor Phil Murphy announced that the acquisition of the right-of-way had been completed. This album was taken on two separate segments of the branch. The northern segment can be accessed behind Nanina's In The Park in Belleville. The section of the ROW abutting the golf course is completely fenced in and blocks access between the northern and southern segments. The southern segment including the large bridge over Mill Street can be accessed in at least two spots in Newark: from behind the baseball fields in Branch Brook Park and from the area near Manchester Place, Highland Avenue and Greenwood Lake Street.

title: All Nintendo Power Covers 1987-2014
description: Here is a slightly new version of every single Nintendo Power cover. (For those wondering about the British ONM covers at the end, Future kept my subscription going by sending me, and a handful of other NP subscribers, issues of the UK magazine until it folded. This is due to have signed up for a three year subscription when I renewed in early 2012. After the last issue of ONM was sent, I also received issues of Edge to finish out my subscription.) Information at the end of the video on how to subscribe. I have no interest with either magazines, other than I’m friends with the publishers. Barely. I also subscribe to both. Feel free to support us, too at https://www.patreon.com/coffee4binky and please consider supporting both magazines via print subscription at https://www.patreonpatreon.com/purenintendo and https://www.patreon.com/nintendoforce and thank you for watching.

title: Episode 77 | Pet Sematary (2019)
description: Welcome to “The Horror of Babylon”, your favorite horror book club podcast. Join us as we delve into the depths of literature’s most warped minds: King, Stoker, Shelley, Ito, Matheson, Koontz, Jackson, and many more. With us, you will face demons that lie in wait and experience nightmares that shall leave you sleepless. Do you have the courage to enter our circle? If not, will you bow to our threats? We think you will… Today we discuss Pet Sematary 2019. Join our community: Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/603336564198002 Discord Group: https://discord.gg/rwGkXNE9xw The Crew: Host the 1st Daniel (@Dstarsick on Twitter) Host the 2nd  Ryan (@DarthDaimyo on YouTube) Host the 3rd Hef (What’s a Twitter?) Producer: Sheldon Executive Producer: Leonard Sound: Micu-san This show is brought to you by our patrons: Abigail the First, Breaker of Chains, & Mother of Dragons Logan II, The Full Metal Patron Four Horsemen Comics & Gaming - Visit 4HM in Morgantown, WV, or Pittsburgh, PA You can support the show at patreon.com/thehorrorofbabylon Our Socials: Find the podcast on Twitter @HorrorPod69.  You can also follow us on Facebook, Instagram, Slasher & GoodReads. Enjoy our gaming content on YouTube. Email us thehorrorofbabylonpodcast@gmail.com You can listen to the show on YouTube, Apple Podcasts, Spotify, Google Podcasts, Audible, and any major podcast app. People Who Deserve Thanks: The Horror of Babylon Logo was created by FlawedPalette from Etsy. The opening theme is "Scary Song" by The Church of Sound. The closing theme is "Deer Woman" by Vivek Abhishek. Rankings: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1--GnWfjnzkRfbsD13kA4JYK0rjIvLuIE?usp=sharing And most importantly: No non-human animals were harmed in the creation of this podcast.

title: Proceedings of the Second Digital Naturalism Conference
description: The Second Digital Naturalism Conference ran for all 31 days of August 2019 in the town of Gamboa, Panama. It sought to bring together interaction designers, artists, field biologists, and anyone interesting in finding new ways to explore the natural world. The First Digital Naturalism Conference ran for 6 weeks in the year 2561 (2018) on Koh Lon, Thailand. It was free for participants to join and independently funded by Andrew Quitmeyer, Tasneem Khan, and additional crowdfunding. Most imagery created and collected by the Dinacon 2 Documentary crew (Ananda Gabo, Jorge Medina, José Alejandro Riascos Ramírez, and Nate Walsh), Andy Quit-meyer and the participants themselves. Book compiled and edited by Andrew Quitmeyer and Kitty Kelly

title: hpr3850 :: New Mexico 2
description: Summary: We finish our stay in New Mexico and head to Presidio, Texas. Series: Travel Source: http://hackerpublicradio.org/eps.php?id=3850 Original audio: https://archive.org/download/hpr3850/hpr3850_source.flac We finished our stay in Anthony with a Saturday trip into Las Cruces to see the local market and the museums, more hiking, and a trip to see Native American Petroglyphs. We also went into El Paso to check out the zoo. It was fine, but we confirmed that we don't really like going into El Paso. We are not much on driving in big cities any longer, particularly in a big truck. Then it was time to move on to our next stop, Presidio, Texas. This is so close to the border that a 5 minute walk takes you to the Rio Grande. Links: https://flic.kr/s/aHBqjzL5vB https://flic.kr/s/aHBqjzL5VY https://flic.kr/s/aHBqjzL7XD https://flic.kr/s/aHBqjzL9jQ https://flic.kr/s/aHBqjzL8cZ https://flic.kr/s/aHBqjzLc5f https://www.palain.com/travel/new-mexico-2/

title: TWIAR 1298 NAT
description: PODCAST: This Week in Amateur Radio Edition #1298 Release Date: January 13, 2024 Here is a summary of the news trending This Week in Amateur Radio. This week's edition is anchored by Chris Perrine, KB2FAF, Patrick Huba. N2WWW, Dave Wilson, WA2HOY, Don Hulick, K2ATJ, Marvin Turner, W0MET, Will Rogers, K5WLR, Eric Zittel, KD2RJX, Michael LaMountain, KE2AWY, George Bowen, W2XBS, and Jessica Bowen, KC2VWX.  Produced and edited by George Bowen, W2XBS. Approximate Running Time: 1:44:43 Trending headlines in this week's bulletin service:   Podcast Download: https://bit.ly/TWIAR1298 Trending headlines in this week's bulletin service  1. QRZ - QRZ Is Hiring Looking For A Software Engineer 2. WBCQ Relies On High-Power Shortwave Antenna 3. WIA: Turkey's First Astronaut Will Communicate with the National Call Sign 4. Congressman Bill Johnson Re-Introduces Amateur Radio Emergency Preparedness Act 5. FCC: FCC Appears Ready To Mandate Disaster Information Reporting System Reporting By Radio Stations 6. The Lost Cosmonauts Of The USSR. Dramatic Transmissions Picked Up From Outer Space 7. The 2024 CQ DX Marathon Is Up And Running 8. ARRL: Marty on the Mountain Engstrom, N1ARY (SK) 9. ARRL: ARRL Responds To FCC Proposals On Removing Symbol Rate Limits 10. ARRL: 2024 Orlando HamCation Awards Are Announced 11. ARRL: ARRL Kids Day A Success In Nebraska 12. ARRL: Winter Field Day 2024 Is Coming Up 13. ARRL: ARISS Is Seeking Educational and Other Organizations To Host ISS Contacts 14. Teen Amateurs Score A Direct Contact With The International Space Station 15. Six Month Extension Is Granted For 160, 6, and 4 Meter Bands For German Amateurs 16. Tom Molyneaux Jr, KE3GK, From East Coast Reflectors, SK 17. Earth-Moon-Earth Conference Is Searching For Presentations 18. ARDC: Ham Radio Village In Colorado Receives Grant For STEM and STEAM Outreach 19. Amateurs Provide Communications And More At Religious Pilgrimage In India 20. Starlink To Enable Experimental Texting Via Space 21. Westchester Emergency Communications Association Hosting General & Extra Classes Online 22. FCC: FCC Set To Wind Down The Affordable Connectivity Program 23. FCC: FCC Chair Asks Automakers About Plans To Secure Car Location Data 24. Upcoming Contests and Conventions 25. ARRL: Teenage amateurs appear on NBC's Today Show During Contact With The Space Station 26. ARRL: HamSci 2024 Workshop is coming up soon 27. Expanded frequency use worldwide expands to include Mobile Base Stations 28. Free online seminars for extra class is offered by The Electronics Museum 29. FCC: FCC opens 2024 hunting in the New York City hotbed of broadcast radio pirates 30. Latest edition of the Volunteer Monitoring Report  Plus these Special Features This Week: * Our technology reporter Leo Laporte, says that the FBI wants you to reboot your router...now! * Working Amateur Radio Satellites with Bruce Paige, KK5DO - AMSAT Satellite News * Foundations of Amateur Radio with Onno Benschop VK6FLAB, will present a talk on The Art of Operating QRP. * The DX Corner with Bill Salyers, AJ8B with news on DXpeditions, DX, upcoming contests and more. * Weekly Propagation Forecast from the ARRL * Bill Continelli, W2XOY - The History of Amateur Radio. This week, Bill takes us aboard The Wayback Machine to March 4th, 1929, to talk about life during The Great Depression and the frivolous hobby of amateur radio. ----- Website: https://www.twiar.net X: @twiar Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/twiari RSS News: https://twiar.net/?feed=rss2 Automated: https://twiar.net/TWIARHAM.mp3 (Static file, changed weekly) -----  Visit our website at www.twiar.net for program audio, and daily for the latest amateur radio and technology news. You can air This Week in Amateur Radio on your repeater! Built in identification breaks every 10 minutes or less. This Week in Amateur Radio is heard on the air on nets and repeaters as a bulletin service all across North America, and all around the world on amateur radio repeater systems, weekends on WA0RCR on 1860 (160 Meters), and more. This Week in Amateur Radio is portable too! The bulletin/news service is available and built for air on local repeaters (check with your local clubs to see if their repeater is carrying the news service) and can be downloaded for air as a weekly podcast to your digital device from just about everywhere. This Week in Amateur Radio is also carried on a number of LPFM stations, so check the low power FM stations in your area. You can also stream the program to your favorite digital device by visiting our web site www.twiar.net. Or, just ask Siri, Alexa, or your Google Nest to play This Week in Amateur Radio! This Week in Amateur Radio is produced by Community Video Associates in upstate New York, and is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License. If you would like to volunteer with us as a news anchor or special segment producer please get in touch with our Executive Producer, George, via email at w2xbs77@gmail.com. Also, please feel free to follow us by joining our popular group on Facebook, and follow our feed on X! Thanks to FortifiedNet.net for the server space! Thanks to Archive.org for the audio space.

title: 2022 Vidya Gaem Awards (Director's Cut)
description: The Director's Cut of the 2022 Vidya Gaem Awards is here! Step into Vidya City as we celebrate the best and worst in gaming this year. 🌆 Battle for Vidya City Synopsis: In a world where video games are the lifeblood of society, Vidya City has been taken over by the sinister Global Acquisitions Group. The city's neighborhoods, each represented by a coveted gaming award, are under the Group's iron grip. Desperate to reclaim their home and restore the spirit of gaming, individual gaming companies band together to form powerful gangs. Each determined to bring good vidya back into the neighborhood and overthrow the evil regime. Patch notes: Improved audio/visual fidelity Better audio mixing The grand return of 2B Radical (thanks Ahch)! "Minor bug fixes" View the winners : https://2022.vidyagaemawards.com/winners View the soundtrack : https://2022.vidyagaemawards.com/soundtrack View the credits : https://2022.vidyagaemawards.com/credits

title: Pleasent Valley Nevada earthquake photos and videos
description: Airel drone videos and photos of the 1915 Pleasent Valley earthquake  in Nevada. These pictures and videos were captured on April 24, 2022; 106 and a half years after the quake had happened. These videos are relased to the public domain for science and seismology. Attached are the raw photographs as well as the JPEGs. The videos are encapsulated in the Matroska video container with the telemetrics stats shown as subtitles. These stats are not actually part of the video, they are captions that can be turned off. There is no audio in the files; video only. The videos are 4k 60FPS and the photographs are 48 Megapixels. Use the Torrent to download faster and save bandwidth.

title: Juneteenth 2022: Lance Brunious
description: A performance by Lance Brunious and band. Recorded at Saint Paul Public Library's George Latimer Central Library. Happy Lance Brunious is an American performer and showman who resides in Saint Paul, MN. Lance sings, dances, and plays trumpet but in the past was I.L.I.C.I.T., an award-winning Twin Cities rapper and touring vocalist. Born in New Orleans, Lance is the son of NOLA-based jazz legend Wendell Brunious. Lance’s grandfather, John “Picket” Brunious, a trumpeter and pianist who studied at Juilliard, arranged for Cab Calloway, Billy Eckstine, and Louis Armstrong. Lance’s late great-uncle, Willy Santiago, is reportedly one of the first guitarists ever recorded in American music and played alongside Buddy Bolden, one of the founding fathers of New Orleans ragtime, the precursor to jazz.

This show is one of the few that I was able to salvage from the Joey After Dark Podcast, and tells the tale of an encounter I had about 5 years ago with a number of the hottest jocks at my gym. This story takes place in the gym steam room. I think you will enjoy it.  If you like  An Older Gay Guy Show Podcast , please consider taking 5 minutes and vote for this show for the Podcast Awards. The instructions for voting are as follows: 1) Go to PodcastAwards dot com  2) Click the big blue box 'Nominations' 3) Fill out the short registration (all your info is deleted the day after the awards) 4) Go to the Nomination Page and scroll down to the  LGBTQ category . 5) Click on  An Older Gay Guy Show 6) Also go to the first category ' People's Choice ' and vote for  An Older Gay Guy Show  in this category as well. 6) Lastly, SAVE your votes at the bottom of the page! title: (2022) Battle City (4 Players) [Dendy]
description: To play normally with sound in the browser using the MAME NES emulator, you need high CPU performance. (2022) Battle City (4 Players) [Dendy] Battle City (4 Players) - модификация игры на основе Battle City (バトルシティー) и Super Tank (超级坦克) 1990 , для эмуляторов и приставок Nintendo Family Computer, Nintendo Entertainment System, Dendy. Внесены изменения; мультиплеер расширен с 2 до 4 игроков одновременно, с соответствующей нагрузкой со стороны противника; добавлены 2 новых бонуса, меню опций и дополнительные 35 уровней. 💾 (2022) Battle City (4 Players) [Dendy] - Cartridge ROM.nes Об игре Игрок должен, управляя своим танком, защищая свой штаб, уничтожить все танки противника на уровне, заодно собирая полезные бонусы и зарабатывая за всё это очки. Если вражеские танки разрушат штаб игрока или уничтожат все его танки, то игра окончена.

title: 2024 Findlay Market Opening Day Parade
description: 2024 Findlay Market Opening Day Parade Thousands turned out for the 105th Findlay Market Opening Day Parade. The parade was led by grand marshals Dmitri Young and Calvin "Pokey" Reese. Cincinnati radio icon Jim Scott served as the parade's honorary grand marshal. Fans along the route saw 17 high school and three college marching bands, and lots of local celebrities, including former Reds announcer Marty Brennaman and former Bengals player Anthony Munoz. Tons of businesses and restaurants had floats in the parade.

title: Disney Channel Through the Years (1983-2020) | Disney Channel Evolution
description: A Youtube video by Alanna Grace, showcasing the evolution of Disney Channel by showing one or more channel IDs a year from the channel's inception in 1983 to the video's creation in 2020. The bumpers are: - 1983: Launch Countdown - 1984: Mickey Satellite - 1985: Stacked Logo/Disney Night Time promo - 1986: Your Magic Carpet for the Finest in Family Entertainment/A Disney Channel Feature Presentation - 1987: Safari/Blinds/Coming up next: Walt Disney Presents - 1988: Model Train Set/Connect the Dots - 1989: Date with Minnie - 1990: Wild West Dark Ride - 1991: Grauman's Chinese Theater Handprints/Flipbook - 1992: Light Tower/Mickey Sun - 1993: Too Many Doors/You're tune in to where the fun is - 1994: Rock Sculpture - 1995: Drumming - 1996: Something for everyone at the Disney Channel online - 1997: Aladdin in the Mickey TV - 1998: Dancing Mickey - 1999: Magic Slide - 2000: We'll be right back/We now return to the show - 2001: Crackling Mickey TV - 2002: Disney Channel Movie intro - 2003: Coming up next: The Jersey and Lloyd in Space - 2004: Coming up next: Toothless - 2005: Disney Channel Original logo from the end of "Zenon: Girl of the 21st Century"/Wand ID with Gonzo from The Muppets - 2006: We'll be right back with Hannah Montana/We now return to Hannah Montana - 2007: Coming up next: Kim Possible/Coming up next: Brandy and Mr. Whiskers - 2008: We'll be right back with The Suite Life on Deck/We now return to The Suite Life on Deck - 2009: We'll be right back with our Disney Channel Movie/We'll be right back with Sonny With a Chance/We now return to Sonny With a Chance - 2010: We'll be right back with Kim Possible - 2011: Coming up next: Fish Hooks - 2012: Coming up next: ANT Farm - 2013: Gravity Falls: Dipper Crashing Through a Billboard/We'll be right back with Liv and Maddie - 2014: Coming up next: I Didn't Do It - 2015: Soccer/We'll be right back with Perman - 2016: Pull-Up Bar/We'll be right back - 2017: Disney Channel Movie intro - 2018: We'll be right back with Bunk'd/We now return to Bunk'd - 2019: Coming up next: Right Now Kapow - 2020: Coming up next: Gravity Falls

title: AOGGS INTERVIEW - Ryan Hunter - Dark Shadows - Part 1- The Original Series
description: I'm so excited to bring you this incredibly fun and funny 3-part series with Radical Ryan Hunter of the  Radical Retro Rewind Podcast  and  YouTube  channel.  In this series, we will cover all things  Dark Shadows , from  The Original Series  to the 1991 re-boot and many, many more  Dark Shadows  memories will be discussed. I have waited literally years to be able to have this kind of discussion about my all-time favorite childhood show. Thank you for joining us! In Part 1, we have an overview of the original series, and then Ryan and I get into our favorite 'likes' and 'dislikes' from that 5 year show.  Parts 2 & 3 will be coming out on June 9th and June 16th, respectively.
